integration_blocked_until: operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with author: operator, status: pending, and approved_exp_ids containing 'EXP-208'
summary: EXP-A3 of the Phase A design refresh is drafted: masthead stance statement. The operator-authored sentence ('The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology. That does not prevent us from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what this document does.') is inserted inside <div class="title-block"> above the existing 'Critical Review' h1, styled as an italicised eyebrow paragraph (0.95rem, serif, ink-2, max-width 56ch, centered). Four test.js assertions lock verbatim text, DOM ordering, serif role, and ink-2 colour. Hero thesis sentence NOT included in this EXP — deferred per Item 1 of the brief. HARD HOLD: do NOT integrate; wait for operator-posted approval HNOTE naming EXP-208. Five further Phase A EXPs (A4–A8) remain after this one, plus a subsequent hero-thesis EXP.
curmudgeon_review_note: This EXP is eligible for adversarial review once drafted. Curmudgeon focus areas: (1) Does placing the stance ABOVE the 'Critical Review' h1 align with the brief's 'above the hero thesis sentence in the masthead' instruction, or is the human review group likely to prefer subsidiary-below placement? (2) Is italic font-style justified for a declarative preamble, or does it risk reading as ironic/undermining on dark backgrounds? (3) Is max-width:56ch the correct editorial cadence, or would full-width (matching subtitle) read more as 'stance' and less as 'preamble'? (4) Are the four load-bearing moves preserved with zero drift from the operator's HNOTE text? (5) Is it correct to defer the hero thesis to a subsequent EXP, or should this EXP include both? Draft must stay DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW regardless of curmudgeon outcome — operator-group review is the gate.
ordering_constraint_note: Recommended integration order: EXP-202 (tokens + webfonts) first, then EXP-208. Not a strict blocker — if EXP-208 is approved while EXP-202 is still held, the stance statement renders via fallback Georgia/Times serif without Source Serif 4 optical tuning. EXP-207 (EXP-A2 typography application) is independent of EXP-208 and does not need to land first.
